messages.content:
**1.2 highlighting why your method, findings, results etc. are important**
Expand the four sentences below, so that the reader understands why something is ‘significant’, ‘interesting’, ‘innovative’, ‘remarkable’ etc. Invent whatever you want.
This method would certainly represent a significant step forward … as it would enable the use of much smaller sample sizes and would thus be around 40% less invasive than current procedures.
1. Our model is very innovative …
**Answer:**
2. These results are very interesting …
**Answer:**
3. This increase in performance is remarkable …
**Answer:**
4. Our findings have wide implications …
**Answer:**

1. **Our model is very innovative** … because it combines real-time physiological signals with patient history in a single, end-to-end framework, rather than treating them as separate inputs. This allows the system to update its predictions dynamically as new data arrive, making it suitable for bedside decision support. In practice, this could replace several sequential analyses with one integrated tool, reducing turnaround time and minimizing opportunities for human error.

2. **These results are very interesting** … as they reveal a clear, previously unreported link between early micro-variations in the biomarker profile and later clinical deterioration. The pattern was consistent across age groups and remained significant even after controlling for medication and comorbidities, suggesting it reflects an underlying mechanism rather than a confound. This opens up a new way to identify high-risk patients hours earlier than standard monitoring permits.

3. **This increase in performance is remarkable** … because it was achieved without adding more sensors, increasing computational cost, or requiring additional patient information. A gain of this magnitude typically demands larger datasets or more invasive measurements, yet our approach improves accuracy while using fewer inputs. As a result, the same clinical team could make more reliable decisions with no extra burden on patients or staff.

4. **Our findings have wide implications** … since the approach can be adapted to multiple conditions where early detection is critical, such as sepsis, stroke, and postoperative complications. Beyond diagnosis, the method could be used to stratify patients for clinical trials, enabling more targeted recruitment and reducing trial size and cost. More broadly, it supports a shift toward earlier, less invasive, data-driven intervention in routine care.

1. **Our model is very innovative** … because it combines two data sources in one simple system, giving accurate predictions without extra tests or equipment.

2. **These results are very interesting** … because they reveal an unexpected pattern that standard analysis misses, which could help detect problems much earlier.

3. **This increase in performance is remarkable** … because the improvement is large even though we used the same amount of data and no additional resources.

4. **Our findings have wide implications** … because the approach can be applied to other diseases and settings, potentially improving diagnosis and reducing cost.

**1.3 comparing the literature**
Complete sentences (a) and (b) so that they mean the same as the preceding sentence.
Example: Many people believe in the existence of a greater force.
(a) A greater force is believed to exist by many people.
(b) It is believed by many people that a greater force exists.
1. The value of these products has been hypothesized as being much higher than previously thought.
(a) It has been hypothesized …
**Answer:**
(b) Values previously estimated for these products …
**Answer:**

(a) It has been hypothesized that the value of these products is much higher than previously thought.

(b) Values previously estimated for these products have been hypothesized as being much lower than their true value.

2. Not all scientists consider that genetic engineering is a good idea.
(a) Genetic engineering is …
**Answer:** 
(b) There is no general consensus …
**Answer:**
3. Some researchers have questioned the fact that global warming is a man-made phenomenon.
(a) Global warming as a man-made phenomenon …
**Answer:**
(b) It has been questioned …
**Answer:**
4. According to some research, homeopathic medicine only has a placebo effect and has no real medical value.
(a) Homeopathic medicine has been found …
**Answer:**
(b) It has been suggested that …
**Answer:**
5. Smith and Jones determined that destiny has a considerable effect on the life of most individuals.
(a) Destiny has been determined to …
**Answer:**
(b) The life of most individuals is …
**Answer:**
6. Irradiation of food products will increase by 180% over the next five years according to Choe et al.
(a) Choe et al. have proposed that …
**Answer:**
(b) It has been …
**Answer:**

2.  
(a) Genetic engineering is **not considered to be a good idea by all scientists**.  
(b) There is no general consensus **among scientists that genetic engineering is a good idea**.

3.  
(a) Global warming as a man-made phenomenon **has been questioned by some researchers**.  
(b) It has been questioned **by some researchers whether global warming is a man-made phenomenon**.

4.  
(a) Homeopathic medicine has been found **to have only a placebo effect and no real medical value**.  
(b) It has been suggested that **homeopathic medicine only has a placebo effect and has no real medical value**.

5.  
(a) Destiny has been determined to **have a considerable effect on the life of most individuals**.  
(b) The life of most individuals is **considerably affected by destiny, according to Smith and Jones**.

6.  
(a) Choe et al. have proposed that **irradiation of food products will increase by 180% over the next five years**.  
(b) It has been **proposed by Choe et al. that irradiation of food products will increase by 180% over the next five years**.

**1.4 stating how a finding is important**
Instead of telling your readers that a finding is interesting or significant, you should show them how it is interesting or significant. Show readers what they need to know to come to their own conclusion about the result. Based on the information given in the first column of the table below, rewrite the sentences in the second column so that this information becomes more explicit and would thus stand out from other information given in the Results/Discussion section. Invent more information if you wish. Do number 1 first (Solid A), then compare your answer with the key (which contains just one possible answer, yours may be quite different). Then copy the style/approach of the key to do numbers 2–4.
|  | YOUR FINDINGS | WHAT YOU SAID ABOUT YOUR FINDINGS |
| --- | --- | --- |
| 1 | When cold, Solid A was hard, brittle and difficult to shape.
When heated to 1,200 °C Solid A became soft and malleable, and changed color.
No one has previously thought of heating Solid A. | Interestingly, the characteristics of Solid A when in a cold state changed radically when heated (see Table 1). |
| 2 | Solar panels take 15 years to become cost-effective, not 10 years as estimated by Sun and Chang [2013].
Producing solar panels consumes ten times more energy and materials than the energy needed to extract petroleum from the sea bed to produce the same amount of power. | It was found that solar panels take 15 years to become effective, rather than the previous estimation of 10 years by Sun and Chang [2013].
In terms of parity of power, the production of solar panels requires considerably more energy than extracting petroleum. |
| 3 | Artifacts recently found at the British Museum reveal that the British Isles were colonized by the Chinese in 8,000 BPE (before present era).
Comparison of modern day Celtic (language spoken in Britain before English) and Chinese reveals substantial similarities in syntax. | Using artifacts from the British Museum, we revealed that the British Isles were previously a Chinese colony. This is supported by a comparison of the Chinese and Celtic languages. |
| 4 | Playing with Barbie dolls is intellectually more productive than playing with Lego.
Brainscans on over 10,000 boys and girls aged between 5 and 8 revealed that brain activity was higher in the girls when playing with Barbie, than in boys playing with Lego.
Implications: boys should be encouraged to play with girls’ toys. | Brainscans on over 10,000 boys and girls aged between 5 and 8 revealed that brain activity was higher in the girls when playing with Barbie, than in boys playing with Lego. |
**1. Answer:**
**2. Answer:**
**3. Answer:**
**4. Answer:**

**1. Answer:**  
Table 1 shows that Solid A behaves in two completely different ways depending on temperature. At room temperature it is hard, brittle and difficult to shape, whereas heating it to **1,200 °C** makes it **soft and malleable** and causes a clear **color change**. Since Solid A has not previously been heated in this way, this result identifies a new, simple processing route for shaping Solid A that was not available with conventional “cold” handling.

**2. Answer:**  
Our cost model indicates that solar panels reach break-even only after **15 years**, i.e., **5 years later** than the **10-year** estimate reported by Sun and Chang (2013). Moreover, to generate the same amount of power, manufacturing the panels requires roughly **10× more energy and raw materials** than seabed petroleum extraction. Taken together, these figures imply that earlier comparisons understated both the payback time and the production burden of solar panels.

**3. Answer:**  
The newly catalogued British Museum artifacts date to around **8,000 BPE** and display manufacturing motifs and inscriptions consistent with early Chinese material culture, suggesting a Chinese presence in the British Isles far earlier than existing settlement models allow. This interpretation is reinforced by linguistic analysis: modern Celtic and Chinese show substantial parallels in **syntactic structure** that are unlikely to be due to chance alone. These two independent lines of evidence (archaeology + syntax) therefore support the claim of a previously unrecognized colonization event.

**4. Answer:**  
Across **10,000+** children aged **5–8**, brainscans show higher overall brain activity in **girls playing with Barbie** than in **boys playing with Lego**, indicating that the Barbie-play condition elicited stronger cognitive engagement in this dataset. Because Lego is typically assumed to be the more “educational” toy, this result challenges that assumption and suggests that boys may benefit intellectually from greater exposure to toys traditionally marketed to girls.
